result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  show       Create a visual representation of the paths in an OpenAPI document.
  transform  Transform an OpenAPI or CSDL document into a JSON/YAML OpenAPI v2/v3 document.
  validate   Validate an OpenAPI document.
  plugin     Create manifest files for an OpenAI plugin [preview]

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  show       Create a visual representation of the paths in an OpenAPI document.
  transform  Transform an OpenAPI or CSDL document into a JSON/YAML OpenAPI v2/v3 document.
  validate   Validate an OpenAPI document.
  plugin     Create manifest files for an OpenAI plugin [preview]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create manifest files for an OpenAI plugin [preview]

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi plugin [options]

Options:
  -m, --manifest <manifest>                                                Path to API manifest file to locate and filter an OpenApiDocument
  --of, --output-folder <output-folder>                                    The output directory path for the generated files.
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Overwrite an existing file
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a visual representation of the paths in an OpenAPI document.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi show [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <openapi>                                                  Input OpenAPI description file path or URL
  --cs, --csdl <csdl>                                                      Input CSDL file path or URL
  --csdl-filter, --csf <csdl-filter>                                       Comma delimited list of EntitySets or Singletons to filter CSDL on. e.g. tasks,accounts
  -o, --output <output>                                                    The output file path for the generated file.
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Overwrite an existing file
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create a visual representation of the paths in an OpenAPI document.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi show [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <openapi>                                                  Input OpenAPI description file path or URL
  --cs, --csdl <csdl>                                                      Input CSDL file path or URL
  --csdl-filter, --csf <csdl-filter>                                       Comma delimited list of EntitySets or Singletons to filter CSDL on. e.g. tasks,accounts
  -o, --output <output>                                                    The output file path for the generated file.
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Overwrite an existing file
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Transform an OpenAPI or CSDL document into a JSON/YAML OpenAPI v2/v3 document.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi transform [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <openapi>                                                  Input OpenAPI description file path or URL
  --cs, --csdl <csdl>                                                      Input CSDL file path or URL
  --csdl-filter, --csf <csdl-filter>                                       Comma delimited list of EntitySets or Singletons to filter CSDL on. e.g. tasks,accounts
  -o, --output <output>                                                    The output file path for the generated file.
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Overwrite an existing file
  -v, --version <version>                                                  OpenAPI specification version
  --metadata-version, --mv <metadata-version>                              Graph metadata version to use.
  -f, --format <format>                                                    File format
  --terse-output, --to                                                     Produce terse json output
  --settings-path, --sp <settings-path>                                    The configuration file with CSDL conversion settings.
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Information]
  --filter-by-operationids, --op <filter-by-operationids>                  Filters OpenApiDocument by comma delimited list of OperationId(s) provided
  -t, --filter-by-tags <filter-by-tags>                                    Filters OpenApiDocument by comma delimited list of Tag(s) provided. Also accepts a single regex.
  -c, --filter-by-collection <filter-by-collection>                        Filters OpenApiDocument by Postman collection provided. Provide path to collection file.
  --il, --inline-local                                                     Inline local $ref instances
  --ie, --inline-external                                                  Inline external $ref instances
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Transform an OpenAPI or CSDL document into a JSON/YAML OpenAPI v2/v3 document.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi transform [options]

Options:
  -d, --openapi <openapi>                                                  Input OpenAPI description file path or URL
  --cs, --csdl <csdl>                                                      Input CSDL file path or URL
  --csdl-filter, --csf <csdl-filter>                                       Comma delimited list of EntitySets or Singletons to filter CSDL on. e.g. tasks,accounts
  -o, --output <output>                                                    The output file path for the generated file.
  --clean-output, --co                                                     Overwrite an existing file
  -v, --version <version>                                                  OpenAPI specification version
  --metadata-version, --mv <metadata-version>                              Graph metadata version to use.
  -f, --format <format>                                                    File format
  --terse-output, --to                                                     Produce terse json output
  --settings-path, --sp <settings-path>                                    The configuration file with CSDL conversion settings.
  --ll, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level to use when logging messages to the main output. [default: Information]
  --filter-by-operationids, --op <filter-by-operationids>                  Filters OpenApiDocument by comma delimited list of OperationId(s) provided
  -t, --filter-by-tags <filter-by-tags>                                    Filters OpenApiDocument by comma delimited list of Tag(s) provided. Also accepts a single regex.
  -c, --filter-by-collection <filter-by-collection>                        Filters OpenApiDocument by Postman collection provided. Provide path to collection file.
  --il, --inline-local                                                     Inline local $ref instances
  --ie, --inline-external                                                  Inline external $ref instances
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information
